title: qt / balance sheet / quantitative tightening | Balance Sheet Update | Plans for Reducing the Size of the Federal Reserve's Balance...
summary: The Federal Reserve announced plans to reduce its balance sheet starting June 1, 2022, by setting caps on reinvestment of principal payments received from securities held in the System Open Market Account (SOMA). Treasury securities caps will start at $30 billion per month and increase to $60 billion per month after three months, while agency debt and mortgage-backed securities caps will start at $17.5 billion per month and increase to $35 billion per month after three months.
